name: Engineered E. coli genetic variants extended C. elegans lifespan
summary: The E. coli genetic-composition study anchors the engineered-microbe aging mechanism side of the track.
statement: A genome-wide E. coli mutant feeding screen identified bacterial genetic changes that extended C. elegans lifespan, with follow-up evidence implicating microbial colanic acid and host mitochondrial dynamics.
population_or_model: C. elegans fed E. coli BW25113 and MG1655 mutants
quantitative_note: The PubMed record reports 29 E. coli mutants that prolonged C. elegans lifespan by more than 10%, 21 mutants active when given only to adults, and 23 mutants validated in the MG1655 strain background.
caveats: The evidence is C. elegans feeding biology, not a mammalian or human therapeutic study.
Bacterial genetic perturbations and colanic acid biology are engineering leads, not ready clinical products.
Worm lifespan extension often fails to translate to human aging outcomes.
